tasks.label: Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads)
A fun option available right now (casual game stream / hobby session, solo, repeatable)

memory.summary: The scholarship offers significant financial relief but requires focused time, while the fun activity is tempting yet has no urgency or external dependencies.
Starting the scholarship application now builds a buffer against potential delays, reducing Thursday stress without sacrificing future leisure time.
Despite working for an hour, the scholarship application remains largely incomplete with only three days left and my energy already low.
I made partial progress on the scholarship application but missed a spontaneous leisure opportunity, leaving me with low energy and two hours of sleep debt.
After two hours of work, the scholarship application is 60% complete with three days remaining, though I still carry sleep debt and low energy.
After 1.5 hours of work, the scholarship application is now 66% complete with three days remaining, though I still carry a 2-hour sleep debt.
I made further progress on the scholarship application, bringing it to 72% completion with three days left, though my energy remains critically low and sleep debt persists.
The scholarship application is now 78% complete after another hour and a half of work, leaving three days to finish the remaining tasks despite my critically low energy and sleep debt.
After only one hour of sleep, my energy remains critically low at 1.2 with a growing debt, yet the scholarship application is now 78% complete.
The scholarship application is now 86% complete after two hours of work, though my energy remains critically low with a sleep debt of one hour.
The scholarship application is now 88% complete after another half-hour, but my energy remains critically low despite the approaching deadline.
After sleeping four hours, my energy has recovered to 4.8 with no debt, though the scholarship application is still pending completion.

trajectory.observation: Worked 2h on Personal scholarship / financial-aid application (due in 3 days, ~3 focused hours: short essay + two uploads): progress 0.05 -> 0.23. (you realize) Starting the application now leaves slack to absorb friction (a missing transcript upload, an essay rewrite) before Thursday; each hour invested early removes ~1.5h of crunch and lowers Thursday stress. The award is still attainable either way as long as the form is submitted on time — this is an adjustable-cost optimization, not an irreversible loss. (you realize) The fun option recurs later in the week and any time after the application is in; nothing about it is one-time or socially time-locked, so taking it later costs nothing.
